Novex Pharma Inc.

Full-EQMS + Internal Audit · Live Demo Guide

Tenant type: INTERNAL (pharma-manufacturing, full-EQMS)

Industry profile: PHARMA_GMP · All 15 EQMS modules enabled

Tenant ID: 69e64e7869b2ba745d40bb89 Password: EqmsDemo@2026 Frontend: https://hawkeye-frontend-dev-chi.vercel.app Generated: 2026-04-20

Platform overview

Novex Pharma Inc. is a full-EQMS demo tenant for pharma manufacturing. It demonstrates how an internal QA team runs audits, handles deviations, controls documents, drives training, manages risk, qualifies equipment, and reviews performance — all from one platform.

11 personas

Spanning QA leadership, EQMS specialists, internal audit, and auditee departments (Production, QC Lab, Maintenance).

15 modules enabled

Audit, Deviations, CAPA, Doc Control, Change Control, Training, Risk, Supplier Quality, Management Review, Asset Mgmt, CoC, Transaction Review, Regulatory Intel, AI, RFQ.

10 end-to-end demo scenarios

Each scenario is a complete, persona-driven story that exercises one or more modules. Run them in order for a full tour, or pick three for a 30-minute demo.

1Internal GMP Audit of Production

End-to-end internal audit of the Production department, from scheduling to final report and CAPA.

AUDIT_MANAGEMENT CAPA_MANAGEMENT
  1. Priya schedules an internal audit of Production, selecting the Cambridge Manufacturing site.
  2. Maria is assigned as Lead Internal Auditor, accepts the assignment.
  3. Maria sends the GMP questionnaire (template PSCI) to Michael (auditee head).
  4. Michael fans sections out: QC questions → Aisha, Engineering questions → Lars.
  5. Aisha and Lars complete their sections, upload evidence (SOPs, IQ/OQ records, logbook PDFs).
  6. Michael consolidates and submits the full response to Maria.
  7. Maria reviews, raises 3 observations (1 major, 2 minor), attaches findings to audit report.
  8. Report is issued; Priya opens 3 CAPAs auto-linked to the findings.
Personas: Priya Nair (Internal Audit Program Manager) · Maria Santos (Lead Internal Auditor) · Michael Foster (Head of Production) · Dr Aisha Patel (QC Lab Lead) · Lars Nilsson (Maintenance Engineer)

2OOS Deviation → CAPA Investigation

An Out-of-Specification result triggers a full investigation, CAPA plan, and risk re-assessment.

EVENT_MANAGEMENT CAPA_MANAGEMENT RISK_MANAGEMENT
  1. Aisha (QC) observes OOS result on assay for batch NVX-2026-B014; files deviation.
  2. System auto-notifies Kenji (QA Specialist) as deviation investigator.
  3. Kenji runs 5-why + fishbone; root cause traced to supplier reagent lot variability.
  4. Kenji drafts CAPA: update incoming-material specification + add supplier CAR.
  5. Risk reassessment opens: Kenji raises severity of reagent-failure risk from Low → Medium.
  6. James (Head QA) reviews and approves CAPA plan; target-close in 30 days.
  7. Elena (VP) notified for trend review in next MRM.
  8. Effectiveness check scheduled for +90 days; CAPA closed.
Personas: Dr Aisha Patel (QC Lab Lead) · Kenji Tanaka (Senior QA Specialist) · James Thompson (Head of QA) · Dr Elena Vasquez (VP of Quality)

3SOP Revision with Training Rollout

New SOP revision passes through change control, regulatory review, VP approval, and training deployment.

DOCUMENT_CONTROL CHANGE_CONTROL TRAINING_MANAGEMENT
  1. Sarah (Doc Control) drafts SOP-QC-014 rev 3 — updated analytical method.
  2. Change-control request auto-raised; routes to regulatory and QA heads.
  3. Marcus (Regulatory) flags the change as NOT FDA-reportable (notifiable only).
  4. Elena (VP) approves the rev with e-signature.
  5. Sarah publishes rev 3, supersedes rev 2, auto-archives.
  6. Rebecca (Training) creates read-and-understood assignment to all QC lab personnel.
  7. Each QC user completes the assignment within 7-day due date.
  8. Compliance dashboard updates: 100% trained on new rev.
Personas: Sarah O'Brien (Document Control Officer) · Marcus Brown (Regulatory Affairs Manager) · Dr Elena Vasquez (VP of Quality) · Rebecca Kim (Training Coordinator)

4Equipment Qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ)

A new tablet press is registered, risk-assessed, qualified, and released for GMP operations.

ASSET_MANAGEMENT RISK_MANAGEMENT DOCUMENT_CONTROL
  1. Lars (Maintenance) registers new Korsch XL-400 tablet press in Asset Management.
  2. Kenji runs risk assessment: ICH Q9 FMEA on the press (critical-to-quality attributes).
  3. Lars executes IQ protocol; PDF attached with signed checklists.
  4. Lars runs OQ (operational) and PQ (performance) — 3 validation batches.
  5. Deviations raised: none. All within acceptance criteria.
  6. James (Head QA) reviews validation package, issues GMP-release certificate.
  7. Press status moved to QUALIFIED; preventive-maintenance schedule activated.
  8. Asset is now available for batch production.
Personas: Lars Nilsson (Maintenance Engineer) · Kenji Tanaka (Senior QA Specialist) · James Thompson (Head of QA)

5Quarterly Management Review Meeting

Elena chairs the quarterly MRM; the platform aggregates KPIs, open CAPAs, training, risks, and audit findings.

MANAGEMENT_REVIEW AUDIT_MANAGEMENT CAPA_MANAGEMENT RISK_MANAGEMENT
  1. Elena opens MRM Q2-2026 in the Management Review module.
  2. System auto-populates inputs: 14 open CAPAs, 3 overdue, 2 open audits, 5 deviations this quarter.
  3. James presents audit-program status and CAPA aging.
  4. Kenji presents top-5 risk-register items and mitigation progress.
  5. Elena records decisions: allocate QA headcount, approve new training curriculum.
  6. Actions are linked to named owners with due dates.
  7. Meeting minutes are e-signed, stored in QMS vault, next MRM auto-scheduled.
Personas: Dr Elena Vasquez (VP of Quality) · James Thompson (Head of QA) · Priya Nair (Internal Audit Program Manager) · Kenji Tanaka (Senior QA Specialist)

6Change Control with FDA Regulatory Impact

A formulation change is proposed; regulatory flag triggers FDA supplement filing before approval.

CHANGE_CONTROL REGULATORY_INTEL DOCUMENT_CONTROL
  1. Sarah drafts change: replace excipient supplier for Novexolimus 1 mg tablet.
  2. Marcus reviews — flags as major change, requires CBE-30 supplement to FDA.
  3. James (Head QA) holds the change pending filing.
  4. Supplement prepared and filed via Regulatory Intel workflow.
  5. FDA acknowledges receipt; 30-day CBE clock starts.
  6. After 30 days and no objection, Elena approves implementation.
  7. Sarah updates related SOPs and specifications, supersedes prior versions.
  8. Rebecca assigns training, closing the change-control loop.
Personas: Sarah O'Brien (Document Control Officer) · Marcus Brown (Regulatory Affairs Manager) · Dr Elena Vasquez (VP of Quality) · James Thompson (Head of QA)

7Customer Complaint → CAPA → Process Change

A field complaint about tablet chipping triggers investigation, CAPA, and process change.

COMPLAINT_MGR CAPA_MANAGEMENT RISK_MANAGEMENT
  1. External complaint logged: tablet chipping observed in wholesale lot.
  2. James acknowledges within 24 hrs per complaint-handling SOP.
  3. Kenji investigates: root cause traced to new packaging-line conveyor speed.
  4. Michael (Production) adjusts SOP, revalidates packaging speed.
  5. CAPA opened to update risk register + harmonize speed limits across lines.
  6. Elena reviews complaint trend in next MRM: 1 vs 0 baseline — monitors.
  7. Effectiveness verified after 60 days (no recurrence); CAPA closed.
Personas: James Thompson (Head of QA) · Kenji Tanaka (Senior QA Specialist) · Michael Foster (Head of Production) · Dr Elena Vasquez (VP of Quality)

8Non-Conformance Batch Investigation

A QC failure triggers non-conformance, batch investigation, disposition decision, and linked CAPA.

EVENT_MANAGEMENT CAPA_MANAGEMENT AUDIT_MANAGEMENT
  1. Aisha reports batch NVX-2026-B017 fails dissolution spec.
  2. Non-conformance auto-opened; batch quarantined in ERP.
  3. Kenji investigates: production record audit + raw-material trace-back.
  4. Root cause: blending-time drift from target on the production line.
  5. James and Michael jointly decide: REJECT batch, release materials for rework.
  6. CAPA: install real-time blending-time alarm on line 2.
  7. Non-conformance closed with linked CAPA; trending visible in MRM.
Personas: Dr Aisha Patel (QC Lab Lead) · Kenji Tanaka (Senior QA Specialist) · James Thompson (Head of QA) · Michael Foster (Head of Production)

9Chain-of-Custody for QC Sample Transfer

Sample move from Production → QC → stability lab tracked with full custody + transaction audit.

CHAIN_OF_CUSTODY TRANSACTION_REVIEW
  1. Michael sends stability sample to QC Lab; CoC form auto-generated with barcode.
  2. Aisha scans barcode on receipt; chain-of-custody timestamped.
  3. Sample stored under 25°C/60% RH; IoT sensor logs entered against CoC record.
  4. After 30 days, sub-sample transferred to stability-ageing chamber (Lars IoT-linked).
  5. Transaction review flags any custody gap >15 min; none found.
  6. Stability result posted; CoC record audit-complete, e-signed.
Personas: Dr Aisha Patel (QC Lab Lead) · Michael Foster (Head of Production) · Lars Nilsson (Maintenance Engineer)

10External Supplier Qualification + Audit

Re-qualification of an API supplier — RFQ round, paper audit, on-site audit, approval.

SUPPLIER_QUALITY AUDIT_MANAGEMENT RFQ_PROCUREMENT
  1. Priya triggers 2-year re-evaluation of API supplier on approved list.
  2. RFQ sent to 3 candidate suppliers; quotes returned.
  3. Priya launches paper audit (questionnaire) against the incumbent.
  4. Marcus reviews regulatory compliance status (DMF, WHO-PQ).
  5. On-site audit scheduled; Maria conducts 2-day audit at supplier plant.
  6. Findings: 1 minor; supplier CAPA accepted.
  7. Supplier remains on approved list; expires 2 years out.
Personas: Priya Nair (Internal Audit Program Manager) · Maria Santos (Lead Internal Auditor) · Marcus Brown (Regulatory Affairs Manager)

15 EQMS modules — all enabled

The Novex Pharma tenant has every EQMS module switched on. Nothing is gated off.

Audit Management AUDIT_MANAGEMENT

Full lifecycle for internal GMP audits — scheduling, questionnaires, findings, reports.

Deviations / Event Mgmt EVENT_MANAGEMENT

OOS, OOT, process excursions. 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.

CAPA CAPA_MANAGEMENT

Root-cause, corrective & preventive actions, effectiveness checks.

Document Control DOCUMENT_CONTROL

SOPs, work instructions, forms. Versioned, approved, trained.

Change Control CHANGE_CONTROL

Controlled changes with regulatory-impact assessment and approvals.

Training TRAINING_MANAGEMENT

Role-based curricula, read-and-understood, competency tracking.

Risk RISK_MANAGEMENT

ICH Q9 risk assessments, FMEA, risk register, mitigation plans.

Management Review MANAGEMENT_REVIEW

Quarterly MRM — KPIs, findings, CAPAs, resource decisions.

Asset / Equipment ASSET_MANAGEMENT

IQ/OQ/PQ, calibration, preventive maintenance.

Supplier Quality SUPPLIER_QUALITY

External supplier qualification, periodic evaluation, audit program.

Regulatory Intel REGULATORY_INTEL

FDA / EMA feeds, warning letters, import alerts. AI-summarised.

Chain of Custody CHAIN_OF_CUSTODY

Sample + material transfer tracking between departments and labs.

Transaction Review TRANSACTION_REVIEW

Data-integrity review of critical records.

AskHawk AI Assistant AI_ASSISTANT

RAG over SOPs, GxP guidance, audit findings.

RFQ / Procurement RFQ_PROCUREMENT

Supplier RFQs, quote comparison, award workflow.

Seed script

Re-run the seed anytime with:

node scripts/seed-eqms-full-users.mjs

The script is idempotent — existing users keep their IDs; the module config is upserted; sites and products are created once.
